name: Sierra del Moral and Valdepeñas Loop
name_original: De Moral de Calatrava a Valdepeñas — circular por la Sierra del Moral
description: Cycle a moderate 44.0-mile (70.9 km) circular route from Moral de Calatrava to Valdepeñas, exploring the paved Sierra del Moral roads.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Moral De Calatrava traverse a landscape characterized by its unique volcanic heritage and extensive agricultural plains. The region features undulating roads that pass through vineyards and olive groves, offering varied terrain for cyclists. The Sierra del Moral provides elevated sections, while routes along the Río Jabalón offer flatter stretches. This diverse topography caters to different road cycling preferences, from endurance rides to routes with moderate climbs. Best road cycling routes around Moral De Calatrava The most popular road cycling route is Pozuelo de Calatrava, Miguelturra y Ermita de Santa Marina — circular desde Almagro, a 53.4 miles (85.9 km) trail that takes 3 hours 25 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers a comprehensive exploration of the surrounding towns and countryside. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Sierra del Moral by Bolaños de Calatrava and Almagro — circular from Moral de Calatrava, a moderate 35.7 miles (57.5 km) path. This route features varied terrain, including sections through the Sierra del Moral and views of historic towns. Local road cyclists also love the From Aldea del Rey to Pozuelo de Calatrava — circular by the Río Jabalón, a 33.8 miles (54.4 km) trail leading through agricultural fields and along the Río Jabalón, often completed in about 2 hours 15 minutes. Road cycling around Moral De Calatrava is defined by its volcanic landscape, extensive agricultural fields, and the presence of the Sierra del Moral. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy rides to moderate challenges. The routes in Moral De Calatrava are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.1 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 400 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Moral De Calatrava's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Moral de Calatrava?
A: There are over 10 road cycling routes available around Moral de Calatrava, offering a variety of experiences. Most of these routes are classified as moderate, with a few easier options for those seeking a less strenuous ride.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Moral de Calatrava?
A: The terrain around Moral de Calatrava is quite varied. You'll find undulating roads, flatter sections through extensive agricultural fields, vineyards, and olive groves, and some challenging climbs, particularly in areas like the Sierra del Moral. The unique volcanic landscape also contributes to the diverse topography.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners or those looking for an easier ride?
A: Yes, there are easier road cycling routes available. For instance, the route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1249647502/bolanos-torralba-y-carrion-de-calatrava-circular-desde-almagro" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bolaños, Torralba y Carrión de Calatrava — circular desde Almagro&lt;/a&gt; is classified as easy, offering a less demanding experience with minimal elevation gain.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes for experienced cyclists in the Moral de Calatrava area?
A: Experienced cyclists will find several moderate routes that offer a good challenge. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1251905687/pozuelo-de-calatrava-miguelturra-y-ermita-de-santa-marina-circular-desde-almagro" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozuelo de Calatrava, Miguelturra y Ermita de Santa Marina — circular desde Almagro&lt;/a&gt;, covering over 53 miles with significant elevation, provide a comprehensive test of endurance. The region's undulating roads and climbs in areas like the Sierra del Moral ensure a rewarding ride.

Q: What natural features will I encounter on road cycling routes around Moral de Calatrava?
A: You'll experience a unique blend of natural features. The region is known for its volcanic landscape, with highlights such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1789437" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro Gordo Volcano&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6832426" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Yezosa Volcano&lt;/a&gt;. Many routes also pass through picturesque agricultural fields, vineyards, and olive groves, and some follow the scenic Río Jabalón.

Q: Can I visit historical sites or towns while road cycling in Moral de Calatrava?
A: Absolutely. Many road cycling routes pass through or near historic towns and cultural sites. You can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2895336" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almagro Main Square&lt;/a&gt;, visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3854730" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of San Pedro (Almagro)&lt;/a&gt;, or discover the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2713622" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Oreto and Zuqueca Archaeological Site&lt;/a&gt;, integrating history with your ride.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in the Moral de Calatrava area?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes around Moral de Calatrava are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1249554970/sierra-del-moral-by-bolanos-de-calatrava-and-almagro-circular-from-moral-de-calatrava" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra del Moral by Bolaños de Calatrava and Almagro — circular from Moral de Calatrava&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1249631234/from-aldea-del-rey-to-pozuelo-de-calatrava-circular-by-the-rio-jabalon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Aldea del Rey to Pozuelo de Calatrava — circular by the Río Jabalón&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Moral de Calatrava?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.1 stars. Reviewers often praise the diverse terrain, the picturesque views of agricultural fields and volcanic landscapes, and the opportunity to combine cycling with visits to historic towns and cultural sites.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Moral de Calatrava?
A: The spring and autumn months generally offer the most pleasant conditions for road cycling in Moral de Calatrava, with milder temperatures. Summers can be quite hot, while winters are cooler but often still suitable for riding on clear days.

Q: Are there any specific viewpoints or scenic spots along the road cycling routes?
A: Many routes offer scenic viewpoints, especially those that traverse the Sierra del Moral or pass through the volcanic landscapes. The extensive agricultural fields, vineyards, and olive groves also provide picturesque backdrops throughout your ride, offering numerous opportunities for scenic stops.

Q: Are there any road cycling routes that explore the volcanic heritage of the region?
A: Yes, the unique volcanic landscape is a defining feature of the region. Routes often provide views of volcanic formations, and you can find specific highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1789437" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro Gordo Volcano&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6832426" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Yezosa Volcano&lt;/a&gt; near some of the cycling paths.

Q: Can I find routes that combine cycling with other activities like visiting natural monuments?
A: Absolutely. Some routes are close to natural monuments such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4596459" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Thermal Baths of the Virgen de las Nieves&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4573401" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente del Caño Cave&lt;/a&gt;, allowing you to integrate short stops to explore these unique features into your road cycling adventure.
tips: In Valdepeñas, there is an impressive statue of Don Quixote dedicated to Alberto Ramírez López. This statue, inaugurated in 2006, is 6 metres high and made of bronze. It is located on Avenida 1º de Julio. The sculpture shows Don Quixote with his arms crossed, riding a tired and thin Rocinante.

In it is the Church of Our Lady of the Assumption, occupies one of the flanks of the Plaza Mayor of the town. In plan it has two Herrerian-influenced naves: the main one and San Lorenzo, which is shorter, and a Mannerist chapel on the gospel side.
In the main nave is the altarpiece, a copy of the one from 1553, destroyed in the Civil War, of which six panels have been preserved that correspond to a pre-Mannerist style prior to Correa de Vivar, which some hypotheses attribute to the Hernando Yánez School ( disciple of Raphael). At the end of this nave, the choir on a pointed arch.
In the nave of San Lorenzo, a work by Gregorio Prieto with the figure of the patron saint, the Virgin of Consolation.

It emerges as a place of social recreation that throughout history has undergone several transformations until reaching its current state.
The surroundings of the Plaza de España is the most important monumental area of Moral. Its four sides are delimited by unique buildings, such as the Parish Church of San Andrés Apóstol, the Town Hall, the old Pósito and some stately homes.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level and terrain like for this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: This circular route is classified as moderate, making it suitable for road cyclists with good fitness levels. The terrain is predominantly paved, offering well-surfaced roads. You can expect a rolling to gently hilly profile with some gentle climbs (typically 1-5% gradients) interspersed with longer flat stretches, rather than consistently steep ascents.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the circular road cycling route from Moral de Calatrava to Valdepeñas?
faqs.answer: Based on the route's distance of approximately 70.9 km and elevation gain of about 346 meters, it typically takes around 2 hours and 54 minutes to complete this circular road cycling route. This is an estimated moving time and does not include any stops you might make.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers characteristic rural Spanish landscapes of Castile-La Mancha. You'll cycle through areas known for vineyards, especially around Valdepeñas. Near Moral de Calatrava, you might see wind turbines in the background. The general atmosphere is often described as "deliciously solitary," providing a peaceful experience away from heavy traffic.

faqs.question: Are there any notable landmarks or points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route passes by a few interesting spots. You'll encounter the monument dedicated to Don Quixote (dedicated to Alberto Ramírez). The route also takes you through the town of Valdepeñas, where you can visit the historic Plaza de España.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: While specific seasonal conditions are not detailed, the region's climate suggests that spring and autumn would likely be the most pleasant times for road cycling. Temperatures are generally milder, and the scenery is often at its best. Summers can be very hot in Castile-La Mancha, which might make cycling less comfortable during midday.

faqs.question: Is the route suitable for families or beginners?
faqs.answer: The route is classified as moderate and requires a good fitness level for road cycling. While mostly paved, its distance of nearly 71 km and moderate elevation gain might be challenging for very young children or absolute beginners. It's best suited for cyclists with some experience.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the route in Moral de Calatrava or Valdepeñas?
faqs.answer: Specific designated cyclist parking areas are not detailed, but both Moral de Calatrava and Valdepeñas are towns where you should be able to find public parking. Look for parking options near the town centers or residential areas, keeping local parking regulations in mind.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: As this is a road cycling route primarily on public roads, there are generally no specific restrictions against dogs. However, dogs would need to be able to keep pace with a bicycle or be carried in a suitable bike trailer, and all local leash laws and waste disposal regulations must be followed, especially when passing through towns or populated areas.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or is there an entrance fee for cycling in the Sierra del Moral or Campo de Calatrava region?
faqs.answer: No, there are no special permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route through the Sierra del Moral or the broader Campo de Calatrava region. The route primarily uses public roads, which are freely accessible.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with any other official trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route briefly intersects with or runs alongside several other paths. You'll find sections that coincide with trails such as the Camino de Moral de Calatrava a Valdepeñas, Cañada Real Soriana Oriental, and Camino de Moral de Calatrava a Membrilla, among others.

faqs.question: Are there places to stop for food, drinks, or accommodation along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the route starts and ends in Moral de Calatrava and passes through Valdepeñas, you will find various options for cafes, restaurants, and accommodation in these towns. Valdepeñas, being a larger town, will offer more choices for rest stops and amenities.
